channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The CIVICUS Global Alliance is offering multiple high-impact opportunities in 2026 for researchers, consultants, evaluators, and interns interested in civic space, human rights monitoring, data analysis, and global civil society strengthening. These roles support the CIVICUS Monitor, one of the world’s leading platforms tracking civic freedoms across nearly 200 countries and territories. These openings are […]

The post CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CIVICUS Global Alliance is offering multiple high-impact opportunities in 2026 for researchers, consultants, evaluators, and interns interested in civic space, human rights monitoring, data analysis, and global civil society strengthening. These roles support the CIVICUS Monitor, one of the world’s leading platforms tracking civic freedoms across nearly 200 countries and territories.

These openings are ideal for candidates passionate about democracy, human rights, international relations, political science, data coding, and global development. They also contribute directly to global reports such as the People Power Under Attack annual publication and other civic space monitoring initiatives.

 

About CIVICUS Global Alliance

CIVICUS is a global alliance of civil society organizations working in over 180 countries. Its mission is to strengthen citizen participation, defend civic freedoms, and amplify voices from the Global South.

Through its CIVICUS Monitor, the organization documents violations of freedoms of association, peaceful assembly, and expression while producing global civic space ratings and analytical reports used by policymakers, researchers, and civil society actors worldwide.

 

1. Civic Space Research Intern (2026)

Overview of the Role

The Civic Space Research Intern will support the CIVICUS Monitor team in strengthening global research collaboration ahead of the People Power Under Attack report. The intern will contribute to:

  • Research on civic space incidents globally
  • Implementation of the CIVICUS Monitor incident tracking tool
  • Data entry and analysis of civic space violations
  • Administrative and coordination support tasks
  • Contribution to annual global reporting processes

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree (completed or near completion) in international relations, political science, or related field
  • Strong written and spoken English
  • Knowledge or experience in human rights research
  • Understanding of international human rights frameworks (association, expression, peaceful assembly)
  • Additional UN language proficiency is an advantage

Application Requirements

  • CV
  • One-page motivation letter
  • Applications must be original (AI-generated applications are not allowed)

Deadline

8 July 2026, 11:59 PM SAST

Start Date

First week of August 2026

 

2. Data Coding Consultant – CIVICUS Monitor & EU SEE Programme

Overview of the Role

This consultancy focuses on coding and structuring civic space data for analysis and reporting. Consultants will:

  • Code civic space incidents from published CIVICUS Monitor updates
  • Enter data into standardized civic space incident tools
  • Support EU System for an Enabling Environment (EU SEE) data analysis
  • Transform qualitative reports into structured datasets for trend analysis
  • Contribute to early warning and civic space monitoring systems

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or current student in international relations, political science, social sciences, or related fields
  • Strong Excel and data analysis skills
  • Experience in human rights research or monitoring preferred
  • Strong English communication skills
  • Ability to work independently and remotely
  • High attention to detail and deadlines

Application Requirements

  • CV
  • Cover letter detailing coding/data experience
  • Proposed hourly and daily rates (USD or ZAR depending on location)
  • Availability per week

Deadline

8 July 2026

 

3. Civic Space Research Consultants (Regional Experts)

Overview of the Role

This consultancy supports drafting and editing CIVICUS Monitor country updates and briefs. Consultants will:

  • Write and edit civic space country reports
  • Analyze trends in human rights and civic freedoms
  • Support monitoring of association, expression, and assembly rights
  • Provide regional expertise (Africa, Asia, Latin America, Middle East, etc.)
  • Contribute to ongoing global civic space documentation

Requirements

  • Master’s degree preferred (Political Science, Law, Sociology, International Relations, Journalism, etc.)
  • Minimum 3 years of experience with civil society or human rights organizations
  • Strong understanding of global civic space trends
  • Regional language skills (French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, etc. depending on region)
  • Strong writing and analytical skills in English
  • Ability to work independently

Application Requirements

  • CV with writing samples
  • Motivation letter specifying regional expertise
  • Budget (hourly/daily rates in USD or ZAR)

Deadline

8 July 2026

 

4. External End-of-Phase Evaluator (Netherlands MFA Partnership 2021–2026)

Overview of the Role

CIVICUS is seeking an independent evaluator or evaluation team to assess its strategic partnership with the Netherlands Ministry of Foreign Affairs under the Power of Voices framework.

The evaluation covers 2021–2026 and includes:

  • CIVICUS Monitor
  • Membership and networks
  • Advocacy campaigns (#StandAsMyWitness)
  • Solidarity actions and global initiatives
  • International Civil Society Week

Key Responsibilities

  • Inception phase design and evaluation framework development
  • Mixed-methods data collection (interviews, surveys, case studies)
  • Outcome harvesting and contribution analysis
  • Global stakeholder engagement (especially Global South actors)
  • Final evaluation report (max 60 pages + annexes)

Requirements

  • Experience evaluating international civil society organizations
  • Knowledge of OECD/DAC or IOB evaluation criteria
  • Expertise in qualitative evaluation methods
  • Strong English communication skills (additional languages an advantage)
  • Independence from CIVICUS-funded programs

Deliverables Timeline

  • Inception report: August 2026
  • Interim briefing: October 2026
  • Draft report: November 2026
  • Final report: January 2027

Deadline

13 July 2026, 23:59 SAST

 

General Notes for All Opportunities

  • All roles are remote or partially remote
  • Strong focus on civic space, human rights, and civil society strengthening
  • Preference for candidates with Global South representation
  • High emphasis on independent, ethical, and non-AI-generated application materials (where applicable)
  • Selected candidates may join CIVICUS preferred supplier lists for up to two years

 

  • More global opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Global Career Opportunity for Young Professionals from Donor Countries
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026 is now accepting applications for one of the organization’s newest global talent initiatives designed to cultivate the next generation of development professionals. This prestigious two-year international programme offers young, high-potential professionals from eligible donor and developed countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on real-world development […]

The post World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Global Career Opportunity for Young Professionals from Donor Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026 is now accepting applications for one of the organization’s newest global talent initiatives designed to cultivate the next generation of development professionals. This prestigious two-year international programme offers young, high-potential professionals from eligible donor and developed countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on real-world development projects across World Bank Group country offices around the globe.

Through an immersive, professionally supported experience, participants will contribute directly to development initiatives while expanding their global perspective, strengthening technical skills, and building careers dedicated to addressing some of the world’s most pressing economic, environmental, and social challenges.

Applications for the United Arab Emirates (UAE) donor cycle are currently open and will close on 15 July 2026.

This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the WBG Explorers Program 2026, including its objectives, programme features, eligibility requirements, available assignments, benefits, application process, and why it represents an outstanding career opportunity for aspiring international development professionals.

What Is the WBG Explorers Program?

The World Bank Group Explorers Program is an international talent programme created by the World Bank Group to connect outstanding young professionals from donor and developed countries with the organization’s mission of ending extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity.

The programme enables participants to gain direct exposure to World Bank Group operations by working on development projects across various sectors while living and working in WBG Country Offices around the world.

Beyond professional development, the programme seeks to develop future leaders who will become lifelong advocates for international development and the World Bank Group’s mission.

Participants contribute meaningfully to projects while gaining valuable international experience that strengthens both technical expertise and leadership capacity.

Objectives of the WBG Explorers Program

The programme has several key objectives:

  • Connect talented young professionals with the World Bank Group’s development mission.
  • Build future global development leaders.
  • Provide practical experience working on international development projects.
  • Strengthen understanding of global development challenges.
  • Create ambassadors who will continue supporting sustainable development throughout their careers.
  • Foster cross-cultural collaboration and international professional networks.

Programme Features

The WBG Explorers Program has been carefully designed to provide a structured and highly practical learning experience.

Participants receive:

Two-Year Extended Term Contract (ETC)

Successful applicants receive a two-year Extended Term Contract (ETC) with the World Bank Group.

The contract allows participants to gain substantial professional experience rather than short-term exposure, enabling them to make meaningful contributions to development operations.

Country Office Placement

Unlike many international programmes that are based at headquarters, WBG Explorers are placed directly within World Bank Group Country Offices around the world.

Participants work alongside experienced professionals implementing projects where development challenges are being addressed firsthand.

Headquarters-based placements are not included in this programme.

Hands-on Development Work

Explorers work on real projects aligned with their academic background, technical expertise, and professional interests.

Assignments may involve:

  • Economic analysis
  • Infrastructure development
  • Agriculture
  • Water resource management
  • Digital transformation
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Investment operations
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Nature finance
  • Inclusive conservation
  • Financial analysis
  • Policy development
  • Regional development initiatives

Participants contribute directly to ongoing World Bank Group operations while learning from experienced development practitioners.

Mentorship

Each Explorer is paired with a:

  • Task Team Leader, or
  • World Bank Group Manager

Mentors provide:

  • Professional guidance
  • Technical supervision
  • Career advice
  • Performance feedback
  • Opportunities for learning and growth

This structured mentorship is one of the programme’s major strengths.

Cohort-Based Learning

The programme follows a cohort model with annual recruitment.

Participants benefit from:

  • Customized onboarding
  • Peer learning
  • Cross-country collaboration
  • Networking with fellow Explorers
  • Shared professional development experiences

Professional Growth

The programme emphasizes continuous learning through:

  • Exposure to multidisciplinary teams
  • Collaboration across World Bank institutions
  • Practical problem-solving
  • International teamwork
  • Global development operations

Learn more about the program features HERE

Funding and Benefits

The WBG Explorers Program is fully funded through a donor-funded Trust Fund.

Participants receive:

  • Two-year paid employment contract
  • Competitive salary
  • Standard Extended Term Contract (ETC) benefits
  • Structured mentorship
  • International work experience
  • Exposure to World Bank Group operations
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Global networking
  • Hands-on experience solving development challenges

Areas of Work

Applicants can apply for opportunities across numerous sectors, including:

  • Economic Policy
  • Economics
  • Finance
  • Investment
  • Agriculture
  • Water Resources
  • Land and Geospatial
  • Digital Development
  • Artificial Intelligence Integration
  • Environmental and Social Development
  • Business Administration
  • Accounting
  • Information Technology
  • Nature Finance
  • Inclusive Conservation

These assignments allow participants to develop specialized expertise while contributing to sustainable development.

Available Duty Stations

The 2026 recruitment cycle includes opportunities in numerous World Bank Group Country Offices worldwide.

Available locations include:

Africa

  • Johannesburg, South Africa
  • Pretoria, South Africa
  • Lagos, Nigeria

Middle East

  • Amman, Jordan

Europe

  • Belgrade, Serbia
  • Bucharest, Romania
  • Tirana, Albania
  • Tbilisi, Georgia

Latin America and the Caribbean

  • Brasilia, Brazil
  • São Paulo, Brazil
  • Bogota, Colombia
  • Buenos Aires, Argentina
  • Panama City, Panama
  • Santiago, Chile

Multi-country Investment Positions

Some investment roles may be based in:

  • Johannesburg
  • Lagos
  • Istanbul
  • Mumbai
  • São Paulo
  • Panama City

depending on organizational needs.

Available Fields

Current opportunities include positions in:

  1. Economic Policy
  2. Land and Geospatial
  3. Digital Development
  4. Water
  5. Economics
  6. Agriculture
  7. AI Integration
  8. Nature Finance and Inclusive Conservation
  9. Investment
  10. Environmental and Social Development
  11. Finance
  12. Business Administration
  13. Accounting
  14. Information Technology

Who Can Apply?

The World Bank Group seeks talented individuals with strong academic backgrounds and a genuine interest in international development.

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Innovation
  • Initiative
  • Professionalism
  • Commitment to development
  • Strong communication skills
  • Analytical thinking
  • Teamwork
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities
  • International mobility

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must satisfy the following requirements.

Education

Candidates must hold:

  • A bachelor’s degree.

Professional Profile

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in development work
  • Self-motivation
  • Sense of urgency
  • Innovation
  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Strong analytical ability
  • Excellent written communication
  • Strong verbal communication skills
  • Ability to work under pressure
  • Team collaboration skills
  • Ability to manage multiple deadlines
  • International mobility

Nationality

Applicants must be nationals of eligible donor or developed countries participating in the programme.

Language Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Fluency in English.

Learn more about the program eligibility HERE.

Programme Timeline

The programme follows an annual recruitment cycle.

Typical schedule:

  • Applications open annually
  • Application review
  • Candidate selection
  • Onboarding around September

Application Deadline

For the 2026 UAE recruitment cycle, applications close on:

15 July 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

How to Apply

Applications are submitted through the Official World Bank Group Careers Portal.

Applicants should:

  1. Review available vacancies.
  2. Identify positions matching their qualifications.
  3. Prepare required application documents.
  4. Submit an online application before the deadline.
  5. Monitor communication regarding recruitment.

Frequently Asked Questions

Applicants often ask about:

  • Programme objectives
  • Eligibility requirements
  • Contract duration
  • Country office placements
  • Benefits
  • Mentorship
  • Assignment process
  • Project types
  • Career progression
  • Funding
  • Application procedures

The World Bank Group provides additional guidance through its official Frequently Asked Questions section.

Why Apply for the WBG Explorers Program?

The WBG Explorers Program provides far more than international work experience.

Participants gain:

  • Direct exposure to global development operations.
  • Practical experience solving complex international challenges.
  • Mentorship from experienced World Bank professionals.
  • Experience working across cultures.
  • Professional networks spanning multiple countries.
  • Opportunities to strengthen technical and leadership skills.
  • A deeper understanding of sustainable development.
  • A strong foundation for future careers in international organizations, government, finance, consulting, and development institutions.

For ambitious young professionals seeking meaningful international careers, the programme offers an exceptional pathway into one of the world’s leading multilateral development organizations.

Visit HERE for more information about the World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026.

Conclusion

The World Bank Group Explorers Program 2026 represents a unique opportunity for talented young professionals from eligible donor and developed countries to launch or accelerate a career in international development. Through a fully funded two-year Extended Term Contract, participants gain practical experience in World Bank Group Country Offices, receive mentorship from experienced leaders, collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, and contribute to projects that improve lives around the world.

With placements available across Africa, Europe, Latin America, the Middle East, and other regions, the programme offers unparalleled exposure to global development challenges while helping participants build the technical expertise, leadership skills, and international networks needed for long-term success.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to review the available positions carefully, identify the opportunities that best match their academic and professional background, and submit their applications before the 15 July 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Job and Early Career Talent Program opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Global Career Opportunity for Young Professionals from Donor Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Calgary Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Political Science: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Canada
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The University of Calgary is inviting applications for the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science, an outstanding research opportunity for early-career scholars seeking to advance their academic careers at one of Canada’s leading research-intensive universities. Hosted by the Department of Political Science within the Faculty of Arts, this prestigious fellowship provides postdoctoral researchers with […]

The post University of Calgary Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Political Science: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Calgary is inviting applications for the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science, an outstanding research opportunity for early-career scholars seeking to advance their academic careers at one of Canada’s leading research-intensive universities. Hosted by the Department of Political Science within the Faculty of Arts, this prestigious fellowship provides postdoctoral researchers with the opportunity to conduct high-quality research, collaborate with renowned faculty members, contribute to teaching and academic life, and expand their professional networks in an internationally recognized academic environment.

The fellowship is designed to support exceptional scholars who have recently completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue innovative research in political science. Through dedicated financial support, mentorship, and access to world-class research facilities, the program aims to foster academic excellence while preparing fellows for successful careers in higher education, research institutions, and public policy organizations.

Application Deadline: Please refer to the official University of Calgary job posting for the closing date and submit your application before the position closes.

About the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship

The Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship honors the legacy of Mark Grosjean by supporting promising scholars whose research contributes to advancing political science. The Department of Political Science at the University of Calgary is recognized for producing impactful research across a wide range of political science disciplines, including comparative politics, Canadian politics, international relations, political theory, public policy, political economy, and quantitative political analysis.

As part of the Faculty of Arts, fellows become members of an active academic community that encourages interdisciplinary collaboration, innovation, and public engagement. The fellowship provides an ideal platform for researchers to strengthen their publication record, develop new research projects, secure future grants, and establish themselves within the international political science community.

What the Fellowship Offers

Successful applicants will benefit from numerous professional and academic opportunities, including:

  • A postdoctoral appointment at the University of Calgary.
  • Access to world-class research facilities and academic resources.
  • Collaboration with experienced faculty members and researchers.
  • Opportunities to present research through seminars and conferences.
  • Professional mentorship and career development.
  • Participation in departmental academic activities.
  • Opportunities to publish research in leading academic journals.
  • An intellectually stimulating and collaborative research environment.

The fellowship is designed not only to support independent research but also to encourage meaningful engagement with students, faculty members, and the wider academic community.

Research Environment at the University of Calgary

The University of Calgary consistently ranks among Canada’s leading research universities. The Department of Political Science offers expertise across numerous research fields, providing fellows with opportunities to collaborate on interdisciplinary projects and contribute to research that informs public policy, governance, democratic institutions, and international affairs.

Researchers benefit from:

  • Modern research infrastructure.
  • Extensive digital and physical library collections.
  • Collaborative research centres.
  • International research partnerships.
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration.
  • Professional development resources.

This environment allows fellows to pursue ambitious research agendas while developing valuable academic leadership skills.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is intended for outstanding early-career researchers who have recently completed a PhD in Political Science or a closely related discipline.

Ideal applicants include scholars whose research interests align with areas such as:

  • Comparative Politics
  • Canadian Politics
  • International Relations
  • Public Policy
  • Political Theory
  • Political Economy
  • Democratic Governance
  • Electoral Politics
  • Public Administration
  • Quantitative Political Science
  • Political Behaviour

Applicants should demonstrate a strong record of academic achievement, high-quality research, publications, conference presentations, and the ability to contribute meaningfully to the department’s research community.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Securing a postdoctoral fellowship is an important milestone in an academic career. Beyond providing dedicated time for research, postdoctoral appointments help scholars develop independent research portfolios, strengthen publication records, secure competitive grants, expand international collaborations, and prepare for faculty positions.

The Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship offers researchers an opportunity to work within a supportive academic environment while contributing to research that addresses important political and societal challenges.

Whether examining democratic governance, public institutions, global politics, or public policy, fellows are encouraged to produce research that advances knowledge and informs decision-making.

Living and Working in Calgary

Located in Alberta, Canada, Calgary is known for its high quality of life, vibrant research community, and strong academic institutions. Fellows benefit from living in a multicultural city with excellent infrastructure, access to nature, and numerous professional networking opportunities.

The University of Calgary provides an inclusive and welcoming environment that values diversity, equity, and academic excellence, making it an attractive destination for researchers from around the world.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should prepare all required application documents as outlined in the official job advertisement. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility requirements, required qualifications, and application instructions before submitting their materials.

Applications should be submitted through the University of Calgary Careers portal.

Application Deadline: Applicants should submit their applications before the closing date indicated on the official vacancy announcement.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

 

The post University of Calgary Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Political Science: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: British Council Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call 2026: Funding Opportunity for International Technical and Vocational Education Partnerships
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The British Council has officially launched the Going Global Partnerships Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Grant Call 2026, providing an exciting funding opportunity for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions seeking to establish or strengthen international partnerships. The grant programme is designed to support collaborative projects between eligible TVET institutions in […]

The post British Council Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call 2026: Funding Opportunity for International Technical and Vocational Education Partnerships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The British Council has officially launched the Going Global Partnerships Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Grant Call 2026, providing an exciting funding opportunity for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions seeking to establish or strengthen international partnerships.

The grant programme is designed to support collaborative projects between eligible TVET institutions in the United Kingdom and partner countries. Through financial support and international cooperation, the initiative aims to improve the quality, relevance, and inclusiveness of vocational education while strengthening skills development that responds to labour market needs and contributes to sustainable economic growth.

The Going Global Partnerships programme reflects the British Council’s commitment to building stronger international education systems by encouraging knowledge exchange, institutional collaboration, innovation, and capacity building across borders.

Application Deadline: Please refer to the official British Council grant page for the application closing date and country-specific deadlines.

About the Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call

Technical and Vocational Education and Training plays a critical role in preparing young people and adults with practical skills that improve employability, support entrepreneurship, and contribute to national development.

Recognising the importance of quality skills education, the British Council developed the Going Global Partnerships programme to foster long-term collaboration between educational institutions, enabling them to learn from each other’s expertise while addressing shared development challenges.

The TVET Grant Call supports collaborative initiatives that strengthen institutional capacity, improve teaching and learning, modernize curricula, promote innovation, and enhance graduate employability.

Rather than funding standalone projects, the programme focuses on building sustainable partnerships that continue generating impact long after the grant period has ended.

Objectives of the Grant Programme

The Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call aims to:

  • Strengthen international institutional partnerships.
  • Improve the quality of technical and vocational education.
  • Enhance skills development aligned with labour market demands.
  • Promote innovation in teaching and learning.
  • Encourage curriculum development and modernization.
  • Support knowledge exchange between institutions.
  • Improve graduate employability.
  • Foster inclusive and equitable access to quality TVET education.
  • Build institutional resilience and long-term collaboration.

These objectives contribute to broader efforts to equip learners with the technical and professional skills needed to succeed in rapidly changing economies.

What Projects Can Be Supported?

The grant encourages collaborative projects that may focus on areas such as:

  • Curriculum review and development.
  • Staff capacity building.
  • Professional development for lecturers and instructors.
  • Teaching and learning innovation.
  • Industry engagement.
  • Employer partnerships.
  • Green skills.
  • Digital skills.
  • Entrepreneurship education.
  • Gender equality and inclusion.
  • Institutional leadership.
  • Quality assurance.
  • Research and policy development.
  • Skills for emerging industries.

Each partnership is expected to design activities that respond to local needs while promoting international best practices.

Benefits of the Grant

Successful institutions may benefit from:

  • Grant funding for approved collaborative projects.
  • International institutional partnerships.
  • Access to global expertise.
  • Knowledge sharing between partner institutions.
  • Improved teaching capacity.
  • Enhanced curriculum quality.
  • Stronger industry engagement.
  • Increased international visibility.
  • Sustainable institutional collaboration.
  • Better employment outcomes for students.

The programme also helps participating institutions strengthen their international networks and create long-term strategic partnerships.

Who Can Apply?

The grant is intended for eligible Technical and Vocational Education and Training institutions participating in the Going Global Partnerships programme.

Applications are generally submitted jointly by partner institutions, including:

  • TVET colleges.
  • Technical institutes.
  • Vocational training institutions.
  • Skills development centres.
  • Further education institutions.
  • Other eligible educational organizations as specified in the official call.

Applicants should establish partnerships that demonstrate shared objectives, institutional commitment, and the potential to create lasting impact.

Eligibility requirements vary depending on the participating country, and applicants should carefully review the official guidance before applying.

Why This Grant Matters

Across the world, employers increasingly require graduates equipped with practical, technical, digital, and entrepreneurial skills.

Strong TVET systems help address unemployment, improve workforce productivity, encourage innovation, and support economic growth.

Through international collaboration, institutions can exchange ideas, adopt successful practices, strengthen curricula, and better prepare learners for modern labour markets.

The British Council’s Going Global Partnerships programme helps institutions achieve these goals by supporting strategic partnerships that improve education quality while fostering international cooperation.

How to Apply

Interested institutions should review the official guidance provided by the British Council, identify an eligible partner institution where required, and prepare a joint project proposal addressing the programme objectives.

Applications should demonstrate:

  • Strong institutional partnerships.
  • Clear project objectives.
  • Sustainable impact.
  • Capacity-building activities.
  • Monitoring and evaluation plans.
  • Alignment with programme priorities.

Applicants are encouraged to submit well-developed proposals before the official deadline applicable to their country.

For complete eligibility requirements, funding information, participating countries, and application guidance, visit the official British Council page:

Discover global grants opportunities on OFY here

The post British Council Going Global Partnerships TVET Grant Call 2026: Funding Opportunity for International Technical and Vocational Education Partnerships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New York State Professional Pathways Program 2026: Paid Career Opportunities for Students and Recent Graduates
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The New York State Professional Pathways Program is now accepting applications from students and recent graduates who are eager to launch rewarding careers in public service. Administered by the New York State Department of Civil Service, this innovative employment initiative provides participants with paid opportunities to gain valuable professional experience while working in New York […]

The post New York State Professional Pathways Program 2026: Paid Career Opportunities for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New York State Professional Pathways Program is now accepting applications from students and recent graduates who are eager to launch rewarding careers in public service. Administered by the New York State Department of Civil Service, this innovative employment initiative provides participants with paid opportunities to gain valuable professional experience while working in New York State government agencies.

The Professional Pathways Program was created to attract talented individuals from diverse educational backgrounds and equip them with practical workplace experience, mentorship, and career development opportunities. Participants have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful public service projects while building the skills and experience needed for long-term careers in government and other professional sectors.

Whether your interests lie in administration, communications, finance, information technology, engineering, environmental sciences, human resources, public policy, legal services, healthcare, or many other disciplines, the Professional Pathways Program offers a gateway to meaningful employment with New York State.

Applications are accepted through available job postings, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible because vacancies may close once positions are filled.

About the Professional Pathways Program

The New York State Professional Pathways Program is designed to strengthen the state’s workforce by recruiting talented students and recent graduates into public service careers. The program recognizes that many graduates face challenges obtaining professional experience immediately after completing their studies.

Through structured employment opportunities, participants gain hands-on experience working alongside experienced professionals in state agencies while contributing to projects that improve services for New York residents.

The initiative also supports workforce development by helping agencies recruit skilled professionals who can contribute innovative ideas, technical expertise, and fresh perspectives.

Unlike traditional internships, many Professional Pathways positions provide competitive salaries, employee benefits where applicable, structured supervision, and opportunities for career advancement within New York State government.

Benefits of Joining the Professional Pathways Program

Participants enjoy numerous professional and career development benefits, including:

  • Paid employment opportunities.
  • Practical professional experience.
  • Mentorship from experienced government professionals.
  • Exposure to public administration and policymaking.
  • Career development and professional networking.
  • Opportunities to develop leadership and communication skills.
  • Experience working on projects that benefit communities.
  • Potential pathways to long-term employment within New York State government.
  • Access to professional training and workplace learning.
  • Opportunities across multiple career fields.

The program allows participants to apply classroom knowledge to real-world challenges while strengthening their resumes and expanding their professional networks.

Career Fields Available

Professional Pathways positions may be available across a wide range of disciplines, including:

  • Business Administration
  • Accounting and Finance
  • Economics
  • Human Resources
  • Information Technology
  • Cybersecurity
  • Data Analysis
  • Communications
  • Public Relations
  • Marketing
  • Public Policy
  • Engineering
  • Environmental Science
  • Health Administration
  • Legal Services
  • Research
  • Project Management
  • Social Services
  • Planning
  • Operations Management
  • Administrative Support

Available positions vary throughout the year depending on agency needs.

Who Can Apply?

The program is intended for:

  • Current undergraduate students.
  • Graduate students.
  • Recent graduates.
  • Individuals seeking entry-level professional experience.
  • Candidates interested in careers in public service.

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong academic preparation.
  • Good communication skills.
  • Professionalism.
  • Interest in serving the public.
  • Ability to work collaboratively.
  • Commitment to learning and professional growth.

Individual vacancies may include additional educational or technical requirements depending on the role.

Why Choose a Career in Public Service?

Working in public service offers opportunities to make meaningful contributions that positively impact millions of people.

Employees help develop policies, improve government services, manage public resources, strengthen communities, protect the environment, support education, improve transportation systems, advance healthcare, and contribute to economic development.

The Professional Pathways Program introduces participants to these rewarding careers while providing valuable experience that can lead to permanent employment opportunities.

Professional Development Opportunities

Throughout their appointments, participants may receive opportunities to:

  • Build technical skills.
  • Improve leadership capabilities.
  • Strengthen problem-solving skills.
  • Develop teamwork and collaboration.
  • Learn project management.
  • Gain experience working with multidisciplinary teams.
  • Understand government operations.
  • Expand professional networks.

These experiences prepare participants for successful careers both within and beyond government.

Why This Program Stands Out

The Professional Pathways Program combines professional employment, practical learning, mentorship, and career development in one comprehensive initiative.

Rather than simply observing workplace activities, participants actively contribute to important government projects while developing transferable skills that employers highly value.

The experience gained can significantly improve future employment prospects and provide a strong foundation for long-term career success.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should visit the official New York State Department of Civil Service Professional Pathways webpage to explore current vacancies and submit applications for positions that match their qualifications and career interests.

Applications are submitted online through the official recruitment portal. Because hiring is based on available vacancies, applicants should monitor the website regularly for new opportunities.

Application Deadline: There is no single application deadline. Positions are posted throughout the year, and each vacancy has its own closing date. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

 

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

 

The post New York State Professional Pathways Program 2026: Paid Career Opportunities for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits, Education, Healthcare, and Economic Development Organizations
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Organizations across the world are constantly searching for sustainable funding opportunities to scale their impact, expand their services, and improve the lives of the communities they serve. One of the most influential philanthropic organizations supporting transformative initiatives globally is the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation. The Michael & Susan Dell Foundation is dedicated to unlocking […]

The post Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits, Education, Healthcare, and Economic Development Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations across the world are constantly searching for sustainable funding opportunities to scale their impact, expand their services, and improve the lives of the communities they serve. One of the most influential philanthropic organizations supporting transformative initiatives globally is the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation.

The Michael & Susan Dell Foundation is dedicated to unlocking human potential and creating opportunities for children, families, and communities to thrive. Through strategic grant funding, the foundation partners with organizations that are working to solve some of society’s most pressing challenges in education, healthcare, livelihoods, financial inclusion, and community development.

For organizations seeking grant funding in Africa, India, Israel, and the United States, the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grants Program presents a significant opportunity to secure financial support and collaborate with a globally respected philanthropic institution.

About the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation

Founded by Michael Dell and Susan Dell, the foundation focuses on creating pathways to opportunity for underserved populations. The organization invests in scalable, evidence-based solutions that help improve educational outcomes, enhance healthcare access, strengthen livelihoods, and promote economic mobility.

Every year, the foundation contributes to:

  • Supporting more than 500,000 classrooms with educational tools and resources.
  • Helping approximately 350,000 students earn university degrees.
  • Creating employment opportunities for over one million young adults.
  • Expanding financial services to millions of entrepreneurs and families.
  • Improving healthcare access and outcomes for millions of families.

The foundation works through partnerships with organizations that demonstrate strong leadership, measurable impact, and the ability to scale solutions effectively.

What the Foundation Funds

The Michael & Susan Dell Foundation provides grants to organizations working across several key focus areas.

1. Quality Schools

The foundation supports initiatives designed to improve educational access and quality for students. This includes projects that strengthen schools, improve teaching practices, enhance student outcomes, and expand access to high-quality learning opportunities.

2. Classroom Supports

Funding is available for programs that help teachers, school leaders, and educational systems deliver better learning outcomes. This may include educational technology, literacy interventions, data-driven instruction, curriculum development, and teacher training.

3. University Success

The foundation invests in programs that help students enroll in, persist through, and graduate from higher education institutions. Projects that provide academic coaching, mentoring, student support services, and career readiness programs are particularly relevant.

4. Jobs and Livelihoods

A major area of investment involves helping young people and adults gain skills, secure employment, and build sustainable livelihoods. The foundation funds workforce development programs, vocational training initiatives, apprenticeships, entrepreneurship support, and job placement programs.

5. Financial Services

The foundation supports innovative approaches that improve financial inclusion and expand access to affordable financial products and services for underserved populations.

6. Health Innovation

Healthcare organizations can receive funding for initiatives that improve maternal health, child health, healthcare delivery systems, digital health solutions, disease prevention, and access to quality medical care.

7. Community Development

The foundation also invests in projects that strengthen communities, improve family well-being, and create long-term social and economic resilience.

Regions Supported

The Michael & Susan Dell Foundation currently funds projects across multiple regions, including:

  • Africa
  • India
  • Israel
  • United States
  • Texas-based initiatives

Organizations operating within these regions are encouraged to explore potential funding opportunities that align with the foundation’s strategic priorities.

Examples of Previously Funded Projects

The foundation has funded hundreds of impactful initiatives across various sectors.

Examples include:

  • Youth employment and workforce development programs in Zambia.
  • Healthcare innovations improving maternal and newborn health in East Africa.
  • Educational initiatives supporting literacy and numeracy outcomes in African schools.
  • Student success and graduation support programs in universities.
  • Economic empowerment projects creating employment opportunities for young adults.
  • Workforce training and apprenticeship programs that connect learners to industry needs.

These investments demonstrate the foundation’s commitment to supporting scalable solutions that generate measurable outcomes.

Benefits of Receiving a Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grant

Organizations selected for funding may benefit from:

  • Significant financial support for project implementation.
  • Long-term strategic partnerships.
  • Access to a global network of experts and organizations.
  • Opportunities to scale successful interventions.
  • Enhanced organizational credibility and visibility.
  • Support for data-driven impact measurement and evaluation.
  • Collaboration with experienced professionals focused on social impact.

Who Should Apply?

Organizations that have proven solutions addressing challenges in education, health, economic mobility, workforce development, financial inclusion, and community well-being should consider applying.

Strong applicants typically demonstrate:

  • A clear mission and measurable impact.
  • Evidence-based interventions.
  • Capacity to scale successful programs.
  • Strong leadership and governance.
  • Sustainable implementation strategies.
  • Commitment to improving outcomes for underserved populations.

How to Apply

Organizations interested in securing funding can visit the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation’s Grants Portal to learn more about funding priorities, eligibility requirements, and application procedures.

Applicants should carefully review the foundation’s focus areas and ensure that their proposed projects align with the foundation’s mission of unlocking human opportunity and improving lives at scale.

Since funding opportunities are available throughout the year, organizations are encouraged to monitor the foundation’s website regularly for updates and application guidance.

Final Thoughts

The Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grants Program remains one of the most impactful philanthropic funding opportunities available to organizations working in education, healthcare, economic development, and community transformation. Through its commitment to measurable impact and scalable solutions, the foundation continues to empower organizations that are creating lasting change for children, families, and communities around the world.

For nonprofits, educational institutions, healthcare organizations, and social enterprises seeking substantial funding and strategic support, the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation offers an opportunity to accelerate impact and contribute to a future where more people can access education, health, and economic opportunity.

Deadline:

There is currently no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Michael & Susan Dell Foundation Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits, Education, Healthcare, and Economic Development Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Innovators Driving the Future of Technology
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Foresight Fellowship 2027 is now accepting applications and nominations for its next cohort. This prestigious year-long fellowship is designed to support exceptional early-career scientists, engineers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators who are committed to accelerating technological progress and solving some of humanity’s most important challenges. Organized by the Foresight Institute, the fellowship connects emerging leaders […]

The post Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Innovators Driving the Future of Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Foresight Fellowship 2027 is now accepting applications and nominations for its next cohort. This prestigious year-long fellowship is designed to support exceptional early-career scientists, engineers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators who are committed to accelerating technological progress and solving some of humanity’s most important challenges.

Organized by the Foresight Institute, the fellowship connects emerging leaders with world-class scientists, industry experts, research mentors, investors, and funding opportunities. Participants also gain access to an international network of innovators working across groundbreaking scientific and technological fields.

Unlike many fellowship programs, the Foresight Fellowship does not impose a minimum work requirement. Instead, it focuses on empowering talented individuals by providing mentorship, strategic career support, networking opportunities, technical discussions, and exposure to influential scientific communities.

If you are building innovative solutions in artificial intelligence, biotechnology, neurotechnology, nanotechnology, or other frontier technologies, this fellowship provides an outstanding platform to accelerate your career and maximize your impact.

About the Foresight Fellowship

The Foresight Fellowship is a highly selective, one-year international fellowship that helps promising innovators develop the relationships, knowledge, and visibility needed to make significant contributions to science and technology.

The fellowship recognizes that many exceptional researchers and builders possess tremendous technical ability but often lack access to influential networks, experienced mentors, and strategic career guidance. Through this program, Fellows receive personalized support that helps transform promising careers into globally impactful ones.

After successfully completing the fellowship, participants become members of the Foresight Fellowship Alumni community, enabling them to continue benefiting from collaborations, networking, and future opportunities.

Fellowship Benefits

Successful Fellows receive an impressive range of professional development opportunities throughout the year.

These include:

  • Introductions to senior scientists and internationally recognized researchers.
  • Connections with relevant funders and investors.
  • Access to experienced mentors working in frontier technologies.
  • Paid travel to one Foresight technical workshop.
  • Attendance at one Vision Weekend Conference.
  • Membership in specialized seminar groups.
  • Opportunity to present personal research or technical work.
  • Priority access to Foresight AI Nodes located in San Francisco and Berlin, collaborative physical spaces designed for AI researchers and builders.
  • Opportunity to showcase achievements to the global Foresight community.
  • Quarterly career counseling sessions with leading experts.
  • Personalized mentorship.
  • Long-term access to an active alumni network after completing the fellowship.

One of the unique advantages of the fellowship is its flexibility. There is no minimum workload requirement, allowing Fellows to continue pursuing their research, startup, academic studies, or professional work while benefiting from the program.

Focus Areas

Applicants should be working in one or more of the Foresight Institute’s priority research areas.

1. Secure AI

Projects may include:

  • Computer security
  • AI alignment
  • Secure multi-agent systems
  • AI safety
  • Automation of alignment research

2. Longevity Biotechnology

Relevant areas include:

  • Laboratory automation
  • Biomedical innovation
  • Reversible biostasis
  • Healthcare 3.0
  • Longevity research

3. Nanotechnology

Research topics include:

  • Molecular 3D printing
  • Molecular simulation
  • Advanced molecular modeling software
  • Atomic-scale manufacturing

4. Neurotechnology

Applicants may work on:

  • Brain-computer interfaces
  • Whole brain emulation
  • Neuro-inspired artificial intelligence
  • Advanced neuroscience technologies

5. Existential Hope

This interdisciplinary area includes:

  • Existential security
  • Technological alignment
  • Strategic foresight
  • Worldbuilding
  • Radical technological progress
  • Long-term future planning

What the Fellowship Looks For

The Foresight Fellowship seeks individuals who demonstrate exceptional technical ability together with ambitious long-term vision.

Applicants are expected to possess several of the following qualities.

Strong Alignment with Foresight’s Mission

Candidates should be committed to advancing breakthrough science and transformative technologies capable of improving humanity’s future.

Proven Domain Expertise

Applicants should already demonstrate significant accomplishments through:

  • Academic publications
  • Research experience
  • Laboratory placements
  • Engineering achievements
  • Startup experience
  • Notable technical projects
  • Professional accomplishments

Technical Excellence

Projects should be:

  • Technically rigorous
  • Scientifically sound
  • Feasible
  • Innovative
  • Capable of generating meaningful real-world impact

Ambitious Vision

The fellowship actively seeks individuals with bold ideas capable of reshaping industries and addressing major global challenges.

Applicants should not be afraid to pursue “moonshot” projects that challenge conventional thinking.

Commitment to Continuous Learning

Successful candidates typically demonstrate:

  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Passion for innovation
  • Continuous personal development
  • Willingness to explore emerging technologies
  • Ability to overcome difficult scientific and engineering challenges

An Exceptional Global Network

The fellowship community includes outstanding innovators working across:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Biotechnology
  • Longevity Science
  • Neurotechnology
  • Nanotechnology
  • Space Technologies
  • Existential Risk and Opportunity Research

Current and previous Fellows come from diverse scientific and entrepreneurial backgrounds, creating a collaborative ecosystem where participants learn from one another.

Mentorship from Leading Experts

Fellows receive mentorship from internationally respected experts representing organizations across frontier technologies.

The mentorship program provides opportunities to:

  • Receive technical guidance.
  • Discuss research challenges.
  • Expand professional networks.
  • Explore funding opportunities.
  • Build long-term collaborations.

Career Development Opportunities

Throughout the fellowship, participants also gain access to career counseling sessions led by industry leaders.

These sessions cover topics such as:

  • Career planning
  • Research leadership
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Startup growth
  • Scientific communication
  • Building successful research careers

Recordings from previous counseling sessions remain publicly available, allowing Fellows to continue learning beyond live events.

Why You Should Apply

The Foresight Fellowship is far more than a traditional fellowship.

It is a global innovation community that equips exceptional early-career scientists and builders with the mentorship, exposure, networks, and resources needed to tackle humanity’s biggest scientific and technological challenges.

Whether your goal is launching breakthrough research, building transformative technologies, founding a startup, or advancing frontier science, this fellowship can significantly accelerate your professional journey.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can either:

  • Apply directly for the 2027 Fellowship cohort.
  • Be nominated by another individual.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application highlighting their technical achievements, research experience, ambitious long-term goals, and alignment with the Foresight Institute’s mission.

For additional information or questions, applicants may contact the fellowship team via fellowship@foresight.org.

Deadline

The application deadline was not specified in the available information. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Innovators Driving the Future of Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship 2027: The Complete Guide to This Prestigious International Clean Energy and Climate Change Fellowship for Global South Professionals
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Yale University’s Jackson School of Global Affairs has officially opened applications for the 2027 cohort of the Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship, an exclusive and highly competitive international fellowship designed to cultivate a global community of clean energy and climate change leaders from emerging and developing countries. Anchored by Yale’s International Leadership Center and led by […]

The post Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship 2027: The Complete Guide to This Prestigious International Clean Energy and Climate Change Fellowship for Global South Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Yale University’s Jackson School of Global Affairs has officially opened applications for the 2027 cohort of the Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship, an exclusive and highly competitive international fellowship designed to cultivate a global community of clean energy and climate change leaders from emerging and developing countries. Anchored by Yale’s International Leadership Center and led by a former senior official of the International Energy Agency, this five-month fellowship combines world-class academic engagement, international networking, remote policy learning, and in-person convenings in both New Haven, Connecticut and Paris, France.

This article provides a complete and detailed overview of the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship 2027 — its mission, structure, programme details, leadership, eligibility criteria, selection process, and how to apply.

The Mission of the Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship

The mission of the Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship is to create a community of committed clean energy and climate change leaders from emerging and developing countries. This community is enriched through direct engagement with top Yale faculty and researchers, as well as leading global authorities in the clean energy transition, including experts from the International Energy Agency (IEA) and beyond.

The fellowship recognizes that the individuals best positioned to drive meaningful climate action in the Global South are professionals who are already embedded within their countries’ energy, policy, business, and civil society sectors — and who, with the right exposure, mentorship, and global network, can accelerate their impact as future leaders in the clean energy and climate space.

Who Are the Yale Climate Fellows?

Each year, sixteen young practitioners are selected from across Asia, Africa, the Middle East, and Latin America and the Caribbean to form a new cohort of Yale Climate Fellows. These fellows are drawn from multiple career tracks and share a common passion for becoming future leaders in the clean energy and climate change space, exchanging findings and experiences with other like-minded professionals from across the Global South.

Yale Climate Fellows are intentionally selected to model diversity across several dimensions:

  • Gender
  • Country of origin
  • Professional background

The professional backgrounds represented within each cohort are intentionally broad and multidisciplinary, including:

  • Public servants and government officials
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Energy company and utility professionals
  • Financiers and bankers
  • Journalists
  • Educators and researchers
  • Civil society representatives
  • Legal advisers
  • Legislators

This diversity of professional perspective is central to the fellowship’s design, as it allows fellows to engage with the clean energy transition from multiple angles — policy, finance, technology, communication, and grassroots advocacy — while learning from one another’s distinct vantage points.

Faculty, Partners, and Program Leadership

Fellows in the programme engage directly with prominent Yale faculty as well as a broad network of top global practitioners working on the most pressing issues in the clean energy and climate change agenda.

Program Leadership

The fellowship is overseen by Paul Simons, a Jackson Senior Fellow and retired United States ambassador who has been actively engaged in global energy and climate change policymaking for more than two decades. Since 2020, Paul Simons has been teaching and mentoring Yale students on the clean energy transition, while also actively participating in the COP26 and COP27 international climate negotiation processes.

From 2015 to 2020, he served as Deputy Executive Director and Chief Operating Officer of the International Energy Agency (IEA) — the world’s leading authority for energy and climate change analysis and policy development — where he led outreach activities with emerging economies and coordinated engagement with the G-20.

The fellowship is further supported by a group of seasoned professionals from the Jackson School’s International Leadership Center (ILC). Director Emma Sky and her ILC team provide essential policy guidance and administrative support throughout the duration of the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship.

Program Structure: A Five-Month Global Journey

The Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship is structured around three core components spanning five months, beginning with an in-person orientation at Yale, continuing through a period of remote learning, and concluding with an in-person closing week in Paris.

Phase 1: Yale Orientation Week (February 1-5, 2027)

The fellowship begins with a one-week, in-person orientation programme at Yale University in New Haven, Connecticut, running from February 1 to February 5, 2027.

During orientation week, fellows will:

  • Engage in a series of interactive sessions with prominent Yale faculty and practitioners to help set the terms of the global climate change debate
  • Explore the wide range of climate change initiatives and research housed across Yale University
  • Delve into cutting-edge scholarship spanning a broad range of clean energy and climate issues

Among the centers and institutions that may be featured during the orientation programme are:

  • The Yale Program on Climate Change Communication
  • The Yale Center for Natural Carbon Capture
  • The Yale Open Lab
  • The Yale Center for Business and the Environment
  • The Yale Center for Environmental Law and Policy
  • The Yale Office of Sustainability, which manages the University’s net zero goal

Phase 2: Remote Learning Journeys (March-June 2027)

From March through June 2027, Climate Fellows will organize and participate in a series of interactive remote learning sessions with leading international experts, covering the full range of policy issues associated with climate change and the clean energy transition. These sessions function as spirited “learning journeys” that allow fellows to develop communities of practice around shared specialties, while benefiting from extensive mentoring opportunities. Sessions typically take place on Wednesdays at 9:00 AM Eastern Time.

Possible topics covered during the remote learning journeys include:

  1. Strengthening global climate change governance, including the United Nations process
  2. Exploring decarbonization pathways, including the IEA Net Zero 2050 scenario
  3. Accelerating renewable electricity growth and addressing the challenges of system integration
  4. Strengthening innovation and uptake of promising clean energy technologies
  5. Promoting energy efficiency and behavioral change
  6. Investigating the challenges of a people-centered clean energy transition
  7. Developing and deepening financial vehicles to advance the clean energy transition
  8. Exploring market-based climate policy tools, including carbon pricing
  9. Exploring regulatory policy tools to support the uptake of clean energy solutions
  10. Improving climate change communication
  11. Closing energy access gaps with clean energy technologies
  12. Developing cost-effective negative emissions technologies, including direct air capture
  13. Exploring the role of nature-based climate solutions
  14. Broadening and deepening corporate and investor initiatives on climate change
  15. Tackling energy security issues, including the role of fossil fuels in the energy transition
  16. Exploring supply chain and related geopolitical challenges of the clean energy transition
  17. Analyzing the challenges of reducing methane emissions in emerging and developing countries

In addition to these remote sessions, fellows will work collaboratively on a group project between March and June 2027.

Phase 3: Paris Closing Week (June 14-18, 2027)

The fellowship concludes with an in-person closing week in Paris, France, running from June 14 to June 18, 2027.

During this final week, fellows will:

  • Meet with top international climate change analysts and policymakers
  • Share perspectives gleaned throughout the entirety of the programme
  • Consolidate professional and personal contacts developed through peer learning and exchanges of views

Fellows will also have valuable networking opportunities with:

  • Staff members from the International Energy Agency (IEA)
  • Representatives from the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)
  • French central government and municipal officials
  • Representatives of civil society organizations, think tanks, academia, and the private sector

As the culminating activity of the fellowship, fellows will present policy recommendations addressing specific clean energy and climate-related challenges to a global advisory board. These findings will then be published, and fellows will be invited to maintain ongoing contacts through the programme’s alumni network following the conclusion of the fellowship.

Find more program details HERE.

Eligibility and Application Requirements

Admission to the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship is highly competitive. Each cohort is intentionally geographically balanced and represents a wide range of professions, talents, experience, cultures, and perspectives. To be eligible to apply, candidates must meet the following requirements:

  1. Be between five and ten years into their professional career, with demonstrated accomplishments at a regional, national, or international level. There is no minimum or maximum age requirement, but the programme is seeking leaders who are still on the rise in their careers
  2. Be representatives of the Global South, specifically from Africa, Asia, the Middle East, or Latin America and the Caribbean
  3. Be fluent in English, as an excellent command of the English language is essential to successfully participating in the programme
  4. Be available to fully participate in all parts of the programme, including in-person attendance for two full weeks: the Yale Orientation Week (February 1-5, 2027) and the Paris Closing Week (June 14-18, 2027)
  5. Be available to participate in six remote learning journeys, held on Wednesdays at 9:00 AM Eastern Time
  6. Be available to work on a collaborative group project between March and June 2027

Learn More

Selection Criteria

Beyond the baseline eligibility requirements, the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship selection committee evaluates applicants against the following criteria:

  • An established record of achievement in a profession linked to clean energy and climate change
  • Representation across a diverse group of stakeholder categories, including government officials, entrepreneurs, energy company and utility professionals, bankers and financiers, journalists, educators and researchers, civil society representatives, legal advisors, and legislators
  • A demonstrated commitment to making a positive impact at the local, national, or international level
  • Demonstrated promise of a future leadership career at both the national and potentially global level
  • An eagerness to expand and deepen professional networks, and to benefit meaningfully from exposure to top global leaders in the clean energy and climate change space

Learn More

Application Process Overview

The application process for the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship is conducted entirely online. There are no paper forms to complete or mail in.

Key details of the application process include:

  • As of June 2026, an electronic application fee is required to complete submission of the application
  • Applicants may work on their application at any time prior to the deadline and submit it whenever they are ready
  • After creating an account and accessing the online application portal, applicants can upload required materials and request their letters of recommendation directly through the system

Required Application Materials

Applicants must submit the following as part of their complete application:

  1. A résumé or curriculum vitae, with a maximum length of three pages
  2. A brief video statement
  3. A personal statement, with a maximum length of 600 words
  4. One letter of recommendation

Apply through the link here: Online Application

Travel and Lodging Coverage

For fellows ultimately selected to the programme, travel and lodging costs for both the Yale Orientation Week and the Paris Closing Week will be fully covered by the programme. This includes:

  • Round-trip airfare
  • Hotel accommodation
  • Transfers
  • Most meals during both in-person weeks

Application Deadline

Applications for the 2027 cohort of the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship close on Thursday, 30 July 2026, at 11:00 AM Eastern Time. This deadline is strict, and no extensions will be granted under any circumstances.

Applicants are strongly advised to submit their application at least 24 hours before the deadline to avoid any last-minute technical issues or delays.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to review the programme’s Frequently Asked Questions page for additional guidance before submitting their application.

Equal Opportunity Statement

Yale University maintains a formal equal opportunity and nondiscrimination policy that applies to the Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship. Full details of this policy are publicly available on Yale’s official website.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The clean energy transition is one of the defining global challenges of this era, and the countries of the Global South — across Africa, Asia, the Middle East, and Latin America and the Caribbean — are simultaneously among those most affected by climate change and most critical to shaping an equitable and effective global response. Yet professionals from these regions have historically had less access to the elite academic institutions, international policy networks, and high-level convenings where global climate strategy is often shaped.

The Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship directly addresses this gap by bringing accomplished mid-career professionals from the Global South into direct, sustained engagement with Yale faculty, IEA experts, OECD representatives, and a global advisory board — while also fully funding the travel costs associated with the programme’s in-person components. The result is a uniquely positioned cohort of leaders equipped not only with deepened technical and policy knowledge, but with a global professional network and an international platform from which to advance climate action in their home countries and regions.

Learn More

Conclusion

The Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship 2027 offers an exceptional and rare opportunity for accomplished mid-career professionals from Africa, Asia, the Middle East, and Latin America and the Caribbean to deepen their expertise in clean energy and climate change policy while joining a prestigious global network anchored by Yale University, the International Energy Agency, and leading institutions across the climate and energy space. With a thoughtfully structured five-month programme spanning an in-person orientation at Yale, months of remote expert-led learning, and a culminating closing week in Paris, the fellowship offers far more than academic enrichment — it offers a genuine platform for career-defining leadership development and international influence.

With only sixteen fellows selected per year and admission described as highly competitive, prospective applicants should take the application process seriously and begin preparing their materials — including a strong personal statement, a compelling video statement, a concise CV, and a thoughtful letter of recommendation — well ahead of the firm deadline of Thursday, 30 July 2026 at 11:00 AM Eastern Time. For mid-career professionals from the Global South who are passionate about climate leadership and eager to expand their global network and impact, the Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship represents a truly transformative opportunity. Apply early, as no extensions will be granted, and take the next step toward shaping the future of the global clean energy transition.

Visit OFY to discover more global Fellowship, Leadership Development and Climate Action opportunities.

The post Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship 2027: The Complete Guide to This Prestigious International Clean Energy and Climate Change Fellowship for Global South Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026: $5,000 Scholarship Opportunity for Undergraduate Students in Illinois, Massachusetts, New York, Ohio, and Texas
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Students pursuing higher education often face significant financial challenges, especially when working toward degrees in competitive and high-demand fields. To help address these challenges and expand educational opportunities for deserving students, ORIX Corporation USA has launched the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026. This scholarship program is designed to support students who are committed to […]

The post ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026: $5,000 Scholarship Opportunity for Undergraduate Students in Illinois, Massachusetts, New York, Ohio, and Texas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students pursuing higher education often face significant financial challenges, especially when working toward degrees in competitive and high-demand fields. To help address these challenges and expand educational opportunities for deserving students, ORIX Corporation USA has launched the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026.
